description_long: This is a military-style, single-propeller airplane with a streamlined, dark green fuselage and red accent markings along the wings and tail. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, extending symmetrically from the body with no visible winglets. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy, positioned just behind the propeller and ahead of the wing root, suggesting a single-seat configuration. The tail features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The landing gear is visible and appears to be a taildragger configuration, with two main wheels under the wings and a smaller tail wheel. The overall appearance is reminiscent of World War II-era fighter aircraft, with a robust metal construction and utilitarian design.
description: A dark green, single-propeller military-style airplane with red accents, mid-mounted straight wings, and a conventional tail. Features an enclosed cockpit and taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, propeller, and tail, with the cockpit canopy and wing profile clearly visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the propeller, nose, and symmetrical wing span, with landing gear and cockpit canopy visible behind the propeller.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, showing the other side of the fuselage, wing, and tail, with similar features visible.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the tail structure, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the back of the cockpit canopy, with the wings extending outward.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main wheels and a single tail wheel, fixed.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy just behind the propeller.
